name: Sopa De Grao
recipeIngredient: 4 medium garlic cloves, peeled and minced
4 large yellow onions, peeled and coarsely chopped
3 tablespoons peanut, corn, or vegetable oil
1 lb Maine or Eastern potatoes, peeled and coarsely chopped
2 quarts beef or chicken broth (preferably homemade)
1/2 pound pepperoni, chorizo, Portuguese chourico, or linguica, sliced about 1/4 inch thick
1 cup finely chopped fresh spinach (leaves only)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the dried garbanzos in a large heavy kettle and soak overnight with 6 cups of cold water.
2. The next day, add the remaining 2 cups of cold water, bring to a gentle boil, cover, and cook for 4-to-5 hours or until the garbanzos are very tender.
3. In a second large, heavy kettle, stir-fry the garlic and onions in peanut oil over moderate heat for 12-to-15 minutes until lightly browned.
4. Add the potatoes and stir-fry for 2-to-3 minutes. Add thyme, coriander, and bay leaf, turn the heat down to low, and allow to mellow about 10 minutes.
5. Pour in the broth and bring all ingredients to a gentle simmer. Cover and cook slowly.
6. In a large heavy skillet, stir-fry the pepperoni until lightly browned and most of the drippings have cooked out. Drain well and set aside, reserving 1 tablespoon of the drippings.
7. Return 1 tablespoon drippings to the skillet, add spinach, and stir-fry for 2-to-3 minutes over moderate heat until glazed and intensely green. Reserve.
8. When the garbanzos are tender, puree half of them using a food processor for 60 seconds, adding only enough kettle liquid to puree easily.
9. Return the pureed garbanzos to the kettle where they cooked.
10. Puree the potato mixture in two batches using the food processor for 30-to-40 seconds each time.
11. Blend the potato mixture into the garbanzo mixture, add the reserved pepperoni and spinach, cover, and simmer for 30-to-40 minutes until flavors are mellowed.
12. Season to taste with salt and pepper, remove the bay leaf, and smooth with olive oil.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
